payload.description:
# Write initial boot prompt for execute_tactus

## What

The first complete draft of the tool-description / boot-prompt that every MCP client will receive for the new `execute_tactus` tool. Used by the spike to validate models can author Plexus-flavored Tactus Lua from this alone (plus on-demand docs).

## Hard constraint

≤2000 tokens, measured with the target frontier models' tokenizers. (5k is the production ceiling per Epic 8; 2k is the spike's tighter constraint to leave headroom.)

## Required content

- Tool name, one-paragraph description.
- The single most important rule: `local plexus = require('plexus')` then use `plexus.*`.
- Discovery: `plexus.docs.list()` and `plexus.docs.get(key)`.
- Cost / budget: `plexus.budget.remaining()`, `plexus.budget.with{}`.
- HITL: destructive ops auto-approve; `no_confirm = true` to bypass.
- Error semantics: `{ ok, value, error }` shape.
- 2–3 minimal canonical examples (read, predict, evaluation invocation).

## What it must NOT do

- Inline the API for every namespace. Use `plexus.docs.get` for that.
- Inline cookbooks for common workflows. Same.
- Be verbose. Aim for cognitive density.

## Deliverable

`spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/boot_prompt.md` with token counts measured and recorded.
payload.title: Write initial boot prompt for execute_tactus (≤2k tokens)
